title: noMisleadingReturnType
description: Learn more about noMisleadingReturnType

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="TypeScript and TSX" icon="seti:typescript">
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
:::note
This rule belongs to the types domain. This means that its activation will activate the Biome Scanner to scan the files of your project, and enable the type inference engine. Read more about it in the [documentation page](/linter/domains#types)
:::
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.4.11`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noMisleadingReturnType`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`types`](/linter/domains#types)
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noMisleadingReturnType": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Detect return type annotations that are misleadingly wider than what
the implementation actually returns.

Reports when a function's explicit return type annotation is wider than
what TypeScript would infer from the implementation, hiding precise types
from callers.

## Examples

### Invalid

```ts title='invalid.ts'
function getStatus(b: boolean): string { if (b) return "loading"; return "idle"; }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid.ts">/invalid.ts</a>:1:31 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-misleading-return-type">lint/nursery/noMisleadingReturnType</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The return type annotation is wider than what the function actually returns.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>function getStatus(b: boolean): string &#123; if (b) return &quot;loading&quot;; return &quot;idle&quot;; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                              <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">A wider return type hides the precise types that callers could rely on.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Consider using &quot;loading&quot; | &quot;idle&quot; as the return type.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/9810">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/9810</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```ts title='invalid2.ts'
function getCode(ok: boolean): number { if (ok) return 200; return 404; }
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text"><a href="file:///invalid2.ts">/invalid2.ts</a>:1:30 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-misleading-return-type">lint/nursery/noMisleadingReturnType</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">The return type annotation is wider than what the function actually returns.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>function getCode(ok: boolean): number &#123; if (ok) return 200; return 404; &#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong>                             <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">A wider return type hides the precise types that callers could rely on.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Consider using 200 | 404 as the return type.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule is still being actively worked on, so it may be missing features or have rough edges. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/9810">https://github.com/biomejs/biome/issues/9810</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information or to report possible bugs.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

```ts
function getStatus() { return "loading"; }
```

```ts
function run(): void { return; }
```
